content.content: 1→# Subdomain Architecture: BizBio → SnapTap Migration 2→ 3→## Context 4→ 5→BizBio is rebranding to SnapTap. 6→ 7→**Current goal (Phase A):** Get `snaptap.co.za` and `snaptap.me` working 100% — landing page, app, profiles, menus — before touching the existing BizBio setup. 8→ 9→**Later (Phase B, deferred):** Redirect all `bizbio.co.za/*` URLs → equivalent `snaptap.co.za/*` URLs via 301. 10→ 11→Key constraints: 12→- Two separate Nuxt instances during transition: `:3000` (existing BizBio, untouched) and `:3001` (new SnapTap app) 13→- SnapTap domains (`snaptap.co.za`, `snaptap.me`) proxy to `:3001` 14→- Once SnapTap is verified on `:3001`, the `:3000` BizBio instance is replaced with the SnapTap build and Nginx is switched over 15→- Nginx + Nuxt server middleware handle routing via the `Host` header 16→ 17→--- 18→ 19→## Architecture Overview 20→ 21→``` 22→Browser request Nginx Destination 23→────────────────────────── ────────────────── ─────────────────────────────────── 24→ 25→── PHASE A (current goal) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 26→ 27→www.snaptap.co.za/* → Nuxt MW 301 → https://snaptap.co.za/* (canonical) 28→www.snaptap.me/* → Nuxt MW 301 → https://snaptap.me/* (canonical) 29→cards.snaptap.co.za/* → Nuxt MW 301 → https://snaptap.co.za/* (alias) 30→cards.snaptap.me/* → Nuxt MW 301 → https://snaptap.me/* (alias) 31→ 32→snaptap.co.za/ → proxy :3001 → Landing page (index.vue) 33→snaptap.co.za/login → proxy :3001 → Nuxt MW → 301 app.snaptap.co.za/login 34→snaptap.co.za/{slug} → proxy :3001 → Nuxt MW: card → serve directly 35→ menu → 301 menu.snaptap.co.za 36→ 37→snaptap.me/ → proxy :3001 → Landing page (same app) 38→snaptap.me/{slug} → proxy :3001 → Nuxt MW: card → serve directly 39→ menu → 301 menu.snaptap.co.za 40→ 41→app.snaptap.co.za/* → proxy :3001 → Full app (dashboard, login, register…) 42→menu.snaptap.co.za/{slug} → proxy :3001 → [entitySlug] Vue page → catalog viewer 43→ 44→bizbio.co.za/* → proxy :3000 → Existing BizBio app (UNCHANGED for now) 45→ 46→── PHASE B (deferred — after SnapTap verified) ───────────────────────────────────── 47→ 48→bizbio.co.za/* → 301 → https://snaptap.co.za/* 49→www.bizbio.co.za/* → 301 → https://snaptap.co.za/* 50→app.bizbio.co.za/* → 301 → https://app.snaptap.co.za/* 51→cards.bizbio.co.za/* → 301 → https://cards.snaptap.co.za/* 52→menu.bizbio.co.za/* → 301 → https://menu.snaptap.co.za/* 53→``` 54→ 55→**Cutover path:** Once snaptap.* is 100% verified on `:3001`, update Nginx to proxy `:3000`, rebuild the BizBio `:3000` instance with the SnapTap codebase, then implement the Phase B redirects. 56→ 57→--- 58→ 59→## Phase 1 — Cloudflare DNS 60→ 61→Add the following DNS records in Cloudflare. All records must be **orange cloud (proxied)**. 62→ 63→### snaptap.co.za 64→ 65→| Type | Name | Content | Proxy | TTL | 66→|------|-------|----------------|--------|------| 67→| A | @ | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | 68→| A | www | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | 69→| A | app | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | 70→| A | cards | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | 71→| A | menu | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | 72→ 73→### snaptap.me 74→ 75→| Type | Name | Content | Proxy | TTL | 76→|------|-------|---------------|-------|------| 77→| A | @ | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | 78→| A | www | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | 79→| A | cards | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | 80→| A | menu | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | 81→ 82→> **bizbio.co.za DNS** — deferred to Phase B (after SnapTap is verified). bizbio.co.za DNS records remain as-is for now. 83→ 84→--- 85→ 86→## Phase 2 — SSL Certificates (Let's Encrypt via certbot --nginx) 87→ 88→Use `certbot --nginx` to issue standard Let's Encrypt certificates. 89→ 90→> **Cloudflare note:** HTTP-01 challenge (used by `certbot --nginx`) requires that port 80 is reachable directly. If Cloudflare proxying (orange cloud) is enabled for the domains during cert issuance, temporarily set them to **DNS only (grey cloud)** in Cloudflare, issue the certs, then re-enable orange cloud. Alternatively, run certbot while the DNS is still grey-cloud and switch to orange cloud after. 91→ 92→### 2.1 Install certbot and Nginx plugin 93→ 94→```bash 95→sudo apt install certbot python3-certbot-nginx 96→``` 97→ 98→### 2.2 Issue certs 99→ 100→Issue a single cert covering all snaptap.co.za subdomains: 101→ 102→```bash 103→sudo certbot certonly --nginx \ 104→ -d snaptap.co.za \ 105→ -d www.snaptap.co.za \ 106→ -d app.snaptap.co.za \ 107→ -d cards.snaptap.co.za \ 108→ -d menu.snaptap.co.za 109→``` 110→ 111→Then snaptap.me: 112→ 113→```bash 114→sudo certbot certonly --nginx \ 115→ -d snaptap.me \ 116→ -d www.snaptap.me \ 117→ -d cards.snaptap.me \ 118→ -d menu.snaptap.me 119→``` 120→ 121→Certificates are stored at: 122→- `/etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.co.za/{fullchain,privkey}.pem` 123→- `/etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/{fullchain,privkey}.pem` 124→ 125→### 2.3 Auto-renewal 126→ 127→```bash 128→sudo certbot renew --dry-run 129→sudo systemctl enable certbot.timer 130→sudo systemctl start certbot.timer 131→``` 132→ 133→> **bizbio.co.za cert** — deferred to Phase B. 134→ 135→--- 136→ 137→## Phase 3 — Nginx Configuration 138→ 139→Create config files for the SnapTap domains only. The bizbio-redirects config is deferred to Phase B. 140→ 141→### 3.1 SSL params snippet 142→ 143→Create `/etc/nginx/snippets/ssl-params.conf` if not already present: 144→ 145→```nginx 146→ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3; 147→ssl_prefer_server_ciphers off; 148→ssl_session_cache shared:SSL:10m; 149→ssl_session_timeout 1d; 150→add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000" always; 151→``` 152→ 153→### 3.2 `/etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap` 154→ 155→All SnapTap traffic proxied to the new SnapTap Nuxt instance on **port 3001**. The `Host` header must be forwarded so the Nuxt middleware can detect which subdomain is active. 156→ 157→> **Transition note:** Once `snaptap.co.za` is verified working on `:3001`, change `proxy_pass` to `http://127.0.0.1:3000` and reload Nginx. Then shut down the `:3001` PM2 process. 158→ 159→```nginx 160→# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 161→# snaptap.co.za — Primary brand. All subdomains proxy to 162→# the NEW SnapTap Nuxt app on port 3001. 163→# (Change to :3000 after cutover verified) 164→# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 165→ 166→# HTTP → HTTPS 167→server { 168→ listen 80; 169→ server_name snaptap.co.za www.snaptap.co.za 170→ app.snaptap.co.za cards.snaptap.co.za menu.snaptap.co.za; 171→ return 301 https://$host$request_uri; 172→} 173→ 174→# www.snaptap.co.za → snaptap.co.za (canonical) 175→server { 176→ listen 443 ssl; 177→ server_name www.snaptap.co.za; 178→ 179→ ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.co.za/fullchain.pem; 180→ ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.co.za/privkey.pem; 181→ include /etc/nginx/snippets/ssl-params.conf; 182→ 183→ return 301 https://snaptap.co.za$request_uri; 184→} 185→ 186→# All live SnapTap subdomains → Nuxt app 187→server { 188→ listen 443 ssl; 189→ server_name snaptap.co.za 190→ app.snaptap.co.za 191→ cards.snaptap.co.za 192→ menu.snaptap.co.za; 193→ 194→ ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.co.za/fullchain.pem; 195→ ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.co.za/privkey.pem; 196→ include /etc/nginx/snippets/ssl-params.conf; 197→ 198→ proxy_read_timeout 60s; 199→ proxy_send_timeout 60s; 200→ 201→ location / { 202→ proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3001; 203→ proxy_http_version 1.1; 204→ 205→ # Critical: pass Host so Nuxt MW can detect subdomain 206→ proxy_set_header Host $host; 207→ proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; 208→ proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; 209→ proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme; 210→ 211→ # WebSocket (Nuxt HMR in dev, harmless in prod) 212→ proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade; 213→ proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade'; 214→ proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade; 215→ } 216→} 217→``` 218→ 219→### 3.3 `/etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me` 220→ 221→`snaptap.me` serves profiles at the root domain, same as `snaptap.co.za`. All traffic proxies to the same SnapTap Nuxt instance on **port 3001** — the domain-routing middleware distinguishes behaviour by host header. 222→ 223→```nginx 224→# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 225→# snaptap.me — Second profile-serving domain. 226→# Profiles live at snaptap.me/{slug} (same app, different domain). 227→# ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── 228→ 229→server { 230→ listen 80; 231→ server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me; 232→ return 301 https://$host$request_uri; 233→} 234→ 235→server { 236→ listen 443 ssl; 237→ server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me; 238→ 239→ ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/fullchain.pem; 240→ ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/privkey.pem; 241→ include /etc/nginx/snippets/ssl-params.conf; 242→ 243→ proxy_read_timeout 60s; 251→ proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; 252→ proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme; 253→ proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade; 254→ proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade'; 255→ proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade; 256→ } 257→} 258→``` 259→ 260→### 3.4 Enable and reload 261→ 262→```bash 263→sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/ 264→sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/ 265→ 266→sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx 267→``` 268→ 269→--- 270→ 271→## Phase 4 — Nuxt Domain-Routing Middleware 272→ 273→**File:** `server/middleware/00.domain-routing.ts` — **COMPLETE** 274→ 275→The `00.` prefix guarantees this runs **before** `profiles.ts` (Nitro sorts middleware alphabetically). 276→ 277→Key behaviours: 278→- `www.*` → 301 canonical bare domain 279→- `cards.*` → 301 canonical root domain 280→- App paths (`/login`, `/register`, `/dashboard`, etc.) on root domain → 301 `app.snaptap.co.za` 281→- Static card slugs → fall through to `profiles.ts` (served from disk) 282→- Entity slugs (menus) → 301 `menu.snaptap.co.za` 283→- `/templates` in `INTERNAL_PREFIXES` so Nitro's `publicAssets` handler serves templates at `/templates/*` 284→ 285→> **Profiles directory**: Both `00.domain-routing.ts` and `profiles.ts` resolve the profiles directory via `process.env.PROFILES_DIR` (falling back to `cwd/profiles`). file.content: # Subdomain Architecture: BizBio → SnapTap Migration ## Context BizBio is rebranding to SnapTap. **Current goal (Phase A):** Get `snaptap.co.za` and `snaptap.me` working 100% — landing page, app, profiles, menus — before touching the existing BizBio setup. **Later (Phase B, deferred):** Redirect all `bizbio.co.za/*` URLs → equivalent `snaptap.co.za/*` URLs via 301. Key constraints: - Two separate Nuxt instances during transition: `:3000` (existing BizBio, untouched) and `:3001` (new SnapTap app) - SnapTap domains (`snaptap.co.za`, `snaptap.me`) proxy to `:3001` - Once SnapTap is verified on `:3001`, the `:3000` BizBio instance is replaced with the SnapTap build and Nginx is switched over - Nginx + Nuxt server middleware handle routing via the `Host` header --- ## Architecture Overview ``` Browser request Nginx Destination ────────────────────────── ────────────────── ─────────────────────────────────── ── PHASE A (current goal) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── www.snaptap.co.za/* → Nuxt MW 301 → https://snaptap.co.za/* (canonical) www.snaptap.me/* → Nuxt MW 301 → https://snaptap.me/* (canonical) cards.snaptap.co.za/* → Nuxt MW 301 → https://snaptap.co.za/* (alias) cards.snaptap.me/* → Nuxt MW 301 → https://snaptap.me/* (alias) snaptap.co.za/ → proxy :3001 → Landing page (index.vue) snaptap.co.za/login → proxy :3001 → Nuxt MW → 301 app.snaptap.co.za/login snaptap.co.za/{slug} → proxy :3001 → Nuxt MW: card → serve directly menu → 301 menu.snaptap.co.za snaptap.me/ → proxy :3001 → Landing page (same app) snaptap.me/{slug} → proxy :3001 → Nuxt MW: card → serve directly menu → 301 menu.snaptap.co.za app.snaptap.co.za/* → proxy :3001 → Full app (dashboard, login, register…) menu.snaptap.co.za/{slug} → proxy :3001 → [entitySlug] Vue page → catalog viewer bizbio.co.za/* → proxy :3000 → Existing BizBio app (UNCHANGED for now) ── PHASE B (deferred — after SnapTap verified) ───────────────────────────────────── bizbio.co.za/* → 301 → https://snaptap.co.za/* www.bizbio.co.za/* → 301 → https://snaptap.co.za/* app.bizbio.co.za/* → 301 → https://app.snaptap.co.za/* cards.bizbio.co.za/* → 301 → https://cards.snaptap.co.za/* menu.bizbio.co.za/* → 301 → https://menu.snaptap.co.za/* ``` **Cutover path:** Once snaptap.* is 100% verified on `:3001`, update Nginx to proxy `:3000`, rebuild the BizBio `:3000` instance with the SnapTap codebase, then implement the Phase B redirects. --- ## Phase 1 — Cloudflare DNS Add the following DNS records in Cloudflare. All records must be **orange cloud (proxied)**. ### snaptap.co.za | Type | Name | Content | Proxy | TTL | |------|-------|----------------|--------|------| | A | @ | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | | A | www | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | | A | app | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | | A | cards | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | | A | menu | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | ### snaptap.me | Type | Name | Content | Proxy | TTL | |------|-------|---------------|-------|------| | A | @ | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | | A | www | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | | A | cards | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | | A | menu | 154.66.198.20 | On | Auto | > **bizbio.co.za DNS** — deferred to Phase B (after SnapTap is verified). bizbio.co.za DNS records remain as-is for now. --- ## Phase 2 — SSL Certificates (Let's Encrypt via certbot --nginx) Use `certbot --nginx` to issue standard Let's Encrypt certificates. > **Cloudflare note:** HTTP-01 challenge (used by `certbot --nginx`) requires that port 80 is reachable directly. If Cloudflare proxying (orange cloud) is enabled for the domains during cert issuance, temporarily set them to **DNS only (grey cloud)** in Cloudflare, issue the certs, then re-enable orange cloud. Alternatively, run certbot while the DNS is still grey-cloud and switch to orange cloud after. ### 2.1 Install certbot and Nginx plugin ```bash sudo apt install certbot python3-certbot-nginx ``` ### 2.2 Issue certs Issue a single cert covering all snaptap.co.za subdomains: ```bash sudo certbot certonly --nginx \ -d snaptap.co.za \ -d www.snaptap.co.za \ -d app.snaptap.co.za \ -d cards.snaptap.co.za \ -d menu.snaptap.co.za ``` Then snaptap.me: ```bash sudo certbot certonly --nginx \ -d snaptap.me \ -d www.snaptap.me \ -d cards.snaptap.me \ -d menu.snaptap.me ``` Certificates are stored at: - `/etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.co.za/{fullchain,privkey}.pem` - `/etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/{fullchain,privkey}.pem` ### 2.3 Auto-renewal ```bash sudo certbot renew --dry-run sudo systemctl enable certbot.timer sudo systemctl start certbot.timer ``` > **bizbio.co.za cert** — deferred to Phase B. --- ## Phase 3 — Nginx Configuration Create config files for the SnapTap domains only. The bizbio-redirects config is deferred to Phase B. ### 3.1 SSL params snippet Create `/etc/nginx/snippets/ssl-params.conf` if not already present: ```nginx ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3; ssl_prefer_server_ciphers off; ssl_session_cache shared:SSL:10m; ssl_session_timeout 1d; add_header Strict-Transport-Security "max-age=63072000" always; ``` ### 3.2 `/etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap` All SnapTap traffic proxied to the new SnapTap Nuxt instance on **port 3001**. The `Host` header must be forwarded so the Nuxt middleware can detect which subdomain is active. > **Transition note:** Once `snaptap.co.za` is verified working on `:3001`, change `proxy_pass` to `http://127.0.0.1:3000` and reload Nginx. Then shut down the `:3001` PM2 process. ```nginx # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # snaptap.co.za — Primary brand. All subdomains proxy to # the NEW SnapTap Nuxt app on port 3001. # (Change to :3000 after cutover verified) # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # HTTP → HTTPS server { listen 80; server_name snaptap.co.za www.snaptap.co.za app.snaptap.co.za cards.snaptap.co.za menu.snaptap.co.za; return 301 https://$host$request_uri; } # www.snaptap.co.za → snaptap.co.za (canonical) server { listen 443 ssl; server_name www.snaptap.co.za; ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.co.za/fullchain.pem; ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.co.za/privkey.pem; include /etc/nginx/snippets/ssl-params.conf; return 301 https://snaptap.co.za$request_uri; } # All live SnapTap subdomains → Nuxt app server { listen 443 ssl; server_name snaptap.co.za app.snaptap.co.za cards.snaptap.co.za menu.snaptap.co.za; ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.co.za/fullchain.pem; ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.co.za/privkey.pem; include /etc/nginx/snippets/ssl-params.conf; proxy_read_timeout 60s; proxy_send_timeout 60s; location / { proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3001; proxy_http_version 1.1; # Critical: pass Host so Nuxt MW can detect subdomain proxy_set_header Host $host; proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme; # WebSocket (Nuxt HMR in dev, harmless in prod) proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade; proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade'; proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade; } } ``` ### 3.3 `/etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me` `snaptap.me` serves profiles at the root domain, same as `snaptap.co.za`. All traffic proxies to the same SnapTap Nuxt instance on **port 3001** — the domain-routing middleware distinguishes behaviour by host header. ```nginx # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── # snaptap.me — Second profile-serving domain. # Profiles live at snaptap.me/{slug} (same app, different domain). # ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── server { listen 80; server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me; return 301 https://$host$request_uri; } server { listen 443 ssl; server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me; ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/fullchain.pem; ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/privkey.pem; include /etc/nginx/snippets/ssl-params.conf; proxy_read_timeout 60s; proxy_send_timeout 60s; location / { proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3001; proxy_http_version 1.1; proxy_set_header Host $host; proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for; proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme; proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade; proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade'; proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade; } } ``` ### 3.4 Enable and reload ```bash sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/ sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx ``` --- ## Phase 4 — Nuxt Domain-Routing Middleware **File:** `server/middleware/00.domain-routing.ts` — **COMPLETE** The `00.` prefix guarantees this runs **before** `profiles.ts` (Nitro sorts middleware alphabetically). Key behaviours: - `www.*` → 301 canonical bare domain - `cards.*` → 301 canonical root domain - App paths (`/login`, `/register`, `/dashboard`, etc.) on root domain → 301 `app.snaptap.co.za` - Static card slugs → fall through to `profiles.ts` (served from disk) - Entity slugs (menus) → 301 `menu.snaptap.co.za` - `/templates` in `INTERNAL_PREFIXES` so Nitro's `publicAssets` handler serves templates at `/templates/*` > **Profiles directory**: Both `00.domain-routing.ts` and `profiles.ts` resolve the profiles directory via `process.env.PROFILES_DIR` (falling back to `cwd/profiles`). The SnapTap PM2 instance sets `PROFILES_DIR=/var/www/bizbio/ui/profiles` so both processes share the same profiles without requiring a separate copy or symlink on the VPS. --- ## Phase 5 — Landing Page CTA Links **File:** `pages/index.vue` — **COMPLETE** Primary CTA buttons use absolute URLs to avoid the middleware redirect round-trip: ```html Get Started Free ``` --- ## Phase 6 — SEO **robots.txt** — **COMPLETE** (`public/robots.txt`) ``` User-agent: * Allow: / Sitemap: https://snaptap.co.za/sitemap.xml ``` **Canonical tags** — **COMPLETE** Added to `pages/[entitySlug]/[catalogSlug].vue`: ```typescript link: [ { rel: 'canonical', href: `https://menu.snaptap.co.za/${route.params.entitySlug}/${route.params.catalogSlug}` } ] ``` **vcard-generator.js branding** — **COMPLETE** ```javascript text: 'Here are my contact details. Get yours on SnapTap.co.za today!', ``` ### Google Search Console (manual) 1. Add `snaptap.co.za` as a Domain property 2. Add `cards.snaptap.co.za` and `menu.snaptap.co.za` as URL-prefix properties 3. After adding, request indexing on a few key pages to accelerate the transition 4. Monitor the old `bizbio.co.za` property — expect 301 coverage within a few weeks --- ## Phase 7 — Deploy ### 7.1 — SSL Certificates (certbot --nginx) ```bash ssh user@154.66.198.20 sudo apt install certbot python3-certbot-nginx # If Cloudflare proxying is on (orange cloud), temporarily set domains to # "DNS only" (grey cloud) in Cloudflare dashboard, then: sudo certbot certonly --nginx \ -d snaptap.co.za -d www.snaptap.co.za \ -d app.snaptap.co.za -d cards.snaptap.co.za -d menu.snaptap.co.za sudo certbot certonly --nginx \ -d snaptap.me -d www.snaptap.me \ -d cards.snaptap.me -d menu.snaptap.me # Re-enable orange cloud in Cloudflare after certs are issued. sudo systemctl enable certbot.timer && sudo systemctl start certbot.timer ``` ### 7.2 — Nginx ```bash sudo nano /etc/nginx/snippets/ssl-params.conf # paste snippet (3.1) sudo nano /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap # paste config (3.2) — proxies :3001 sudo nano /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me # paste config (3.3) — proxies :3001 sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/ sudo ln -s /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/ sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx ``` ### 7.3 — Deploy SnapTap Nuxt app on port 3001 The VPS deployment layout uses a `current/` release directory: ``` /var/www/snaptap/ui/ ecosystem.config.cjs current/ ← symlink (or directory) pointing to latest release server/ index.mjs ← Nitro entry point public/ nitro.json ``` ```bash # Create directory structure mkdir -p /var/www/snaptap/ui # Clone / pull the repo into a release directory and link as current cd /var/www/snaptap/ui git clone https://github.com/YOUR_ORG/bizbio-ui.git releases/$(date +%Y%m%d%H%M%S) cd releases/$(ls -t releases | head -1) git checkout feature/dynamic-templates-in-vue npm install npm run build # Link the built output as current cd /var/www/snaptap/ui ln -sfn releases/$(ls -t releases | head -1)/.output current # Copy ecosystem config into place (if not already present) cp releases/$(ls -t releases | head -1)/ecosystem.config.cjs . # Start with PM2 pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs --only snaptap-frontend --env production pm2 save pm2 status pm2 logs snaptap-frontend --lines 30 ``` ### 7.4 — Cutover (after verifying :3001) Once snaptap.co.za and snaptap.me are working correctly on `:3001`: ```bash # Update Nginx configs: change proxy_pass from :3001 to :3000 sudo sed -i 's/127.0.0.1:3001/127.0.0.1:3000/g' /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap sudo sed -i 's/127.0.0.1:3001/127.0.0.1:3000/g' /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me sudo nginx -t && sudo systemctl reload nginx # Rebuild the BizBio :3000 instance with the SnapTap changes cd /var/www/bizbio/ui git pull npm install npm run build pm2 reload ecosystem.config.cjs --env production # Stop the temporary :3001 instance pm2 delete snaptap-frontend pm2 save ``` --- ## CORS — BizBio API `api.bizbio.co.za` must allow cross-origin requests from the new SnapTap domains. Add to the API's CORS allowed origins: ``` https://snaptap.co.za https://app.snaptap.co.za https://menu.snaptap.co.za https://snaptap.me ``` > Note: SSR `$fetch` calls run server-side and are not affected by CORS. Only browser-side requests need the header. --- ## Verification Checklist (Phase A — SnapTap only) ```bash # SnapTap canonical redirect curl -sI https://www.snaptap.co.za | grep -E "^HTTP|Location" # → snaptap.co.za # SnapTap landing (must be 200) curl -sI https://snaptap.co.za | grep "^HTTP" # SnapTap app-path redirect (must be 301 → app.snaptap.co.za) curl -sI https://snaptap.co.za/login | grep -E "^HTTP|Location" curl -sI https://snaptap.co.za/register | grep -E "^HTTP|Location" # Slug redirects (replace with real slugs from your data) curl -sI https://snaptap.co.za/{card-slug} | grep -E "^HTTP|Location" # → 200 (served directly) curl -sI https://snaptap.co.za/{menu-slug} | grep -E "^HTTP|Location" # → 301 menu.snaptap.co.za # Subdomain content (must serve correctly) curl -sI https://app.snaptap.co.za/login | grep "^HTTP" curl -sI https://cards.snaptap.co.za/{card-slug}/ | grep "^HTTP" curl -sI https://menu.snaptap.co.za/{menu-slug} | grep "^HTTP" # SSL cert check echo | openssl s_client -connect app.snaptap.co.za:443 -servername app.snaptap.co.za 2>/dev/null \ | openssl x509 -noout -subject -dates ``` --- ## Files Status Summary ### Nuxt Repo (all on branch `feature/dynamic-templates-in-vue`) | File | Status | Notes | |------|--------|-------| | `server/middleware/00.domain-routing.ts` | ✅ Done | Domain routing middleware; `PROFILES_DIR` env var support | | `server/middleware/profiles.ts` | ✅ Done | `/templates` in SKIP_PREFIXES; `PROFILES_DIR` env var support | | `nuxt.config.ts` | ✅ Done | publicAssets for templates | | `pages/index.vue` | ✅ Done | CTA links → absolute `app.snaptap.co.za` URLs | | `profiles/vcard-generator.js` | ✅ Done | Branding text → SnapTap.co.za | | `public/robots.txt` | ✅ Done | Points to snaptap.co.za/sitemap.xml | | `pages/[entitySlug]/[catalogSlug].vue` | ✅ Done | Canonical tag → menu.snaptap.co.za | | `ecosystem.config.cjs` | ✅ Done | Both entries use `./current/server/index.mjs`; `snaptap-frontend` cwd → `/var/www/snaptap/ui`; `bizbio-frontend` explicit cwd → `/var/www/bizbio/ui`; `PROFILES_DIR=/var/www/bizbio/ui/profiles` set on both instances | ### VPS (require SSH access) | Task | Status | Notes | |------|--------|-------| | Cloudflare DNS records | ⏳ Pending | Phase 1 — manual in Cloudflare dashboard | | SSL certs (certbot) | ⏳ Pending | Phase 2 — run on VPS | | `/etc/nginx/snippets/ssl-params.conf` | ⏳ Pending | Phase 3.1 | | `/etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap` | ⏳ Pending | Phase 3.2 — proxy to `:3001` | | `/etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me` | ⏳ Pending | Phase 3.3 — proxy to `:3001` | | Deploy snaptap-ui clone on :3001 | ⏳ Pending | Phase 7.3 | | BizBio API CORS update | ⏳ Pending | Add snaptap.* origins | --- ## Phase B — bizbio.co.za Redirects (Deferred) **Trigger:** After `snaptap.co.za` and `snaptap.me` are fully verified. ### B.1 SSL cert for bizbio.co.za ```bash sudo certbot certonly --nginx \ -d bizbio.co.za -d www.bizbio.co.za \ -d app.bizbio.co.za -d cards.bizbio.co.za -d menu.bizbio.co.za ``` ### B.2 `/etc/nginx/sites-available/bizbio-redirects` ```nginx # HTTP → HTTPS server { listen 80; 1→import { defineEventHandler, getRequestHeader, getRequestURL, sendRedirect } from 'h3'
     2→import { stat } from 'fs/promises'
     3→import { join } from 'path'
     4→
     5→const API_URL = process.env.NUXT_PUBLIC_API_URL || 'https://api.bizbio.co.za/api/v1'
     6→const PROFILES_DIR = process.env.PROFILES_DIR || join(process.cwd(), 'profiles')
     7→
     8→// Root domains that serve profiles directly at /{slug} with no redirect.
     9→// Add additional domains here as the brand expands.
    10→const PROFILE_ROOT_DOMAINS = new Set(['snaptap.co.za', 'snaptap.me'])
    11→
    12→// www subdomain → canonical bare domain
    13→const WWW_TO_CANONICAL: Record<string, string> = {
    14→  'www.snaptap.co.za': 'snaptap.co.za',
    15→  'www.snaptap.me':    'snaptap.me',
    16→}
    17→
    18→// cards. subdomain → redirect back to the canonical root domain
    19→const CARDS_TO_CANONICAL: Record<string, string> = {
    20→  'cards.snaptap.co.za': 'snaptap.co.za',
    21→  'cards.snaptap.me':    'snaptap.me',
    22→}
    23→
    24→// Marketing / landing pages — served as-is on the root domain
    25→const LANDING_EXACT = new Set([
    26→  '/', '/pricing', '/categories', '/help', '/contact',
    27→  '/terms', '/privacy', '/search', '/products',
    28→])
    29→
    30→// App-area paths → redirect to app.snaptap.co.za (the single app home)
    31→const APP_PREFIXES = [
    32→  '/login', '/register', '/forgot-password', '/reset-password',
    33→  '/verify-email', '/dashboard', '/menu',
    34→]
    35→
    36→// Nuxt / system internals — never intercept
    37→const INTERNAL_PREFIXES = [
    38→  '/_nuxt', '/api/', '/__nuxt', '/@', '/favicon',
    39→  '/robots', '/sitemap', '/templates', '/404', '/500',
    40→]
    41→
    42→async function isStaticCardSlug(slug: string): Promise<boolean> {
    43→  try {
    44→    const s = await stat(join(PROFILES_DIR, slug))
    45→    return s.isDirectory()
    46→  } catch {
    47→    return false
    48→  }
    49→}
    50→
    51→async function isEntitySlug(slug: string, apiUrl: string): Promise<boolean> {
    52→  try {
    53→    await $fetch(`${apiUrl}/entities/by-slug/${slug}`, { method: 'GET', timeout: 3000 })
    54→    return true
    55→  } catch {
    56→    return false
    57→  }
    58→}
    59→
    60→export default defineEventHandler(async (event) => {
    61→  const rawHost = getRequestHeader(event, 'host') ?? ''
    62→  const host    = rawHost.split(':')[0].toLowerCase()
    63→  const url     = getRequestURL(event)
    64→  const path    = url.pathname
    65→  const qs      = url.search ?? ''
    66→
    67→  // Never intercept Nuxt internals or templates (served as public assets)
    68→  if (INTERNAL_PREFIXES.some(p => path.startsWith(p))) return
    69→
    70→  // ── www.* → canonical bare domain ──────────────────────────────────────
    71→  const canonical = WWW_TO_CANONICAL[host]
    72→  if (canonical) {
    73→    return sendRedirect(event, `https://${canonical}${path}${qs}`, 301)
    74→  }
    75→
    76→  // ── cards.* → canonical root domain (profiles live at root) ────────────
    77→  const cardsCanonical = CARDS_TO_CANONICAL[host]
    78→  if (cardsCanonical) {
    79→    return sendRedirect(event, `https://${cardsCanonical}${path}${qs}`, 301)
    80→  }
    81→
    82→  // ── All other subdomains (app.*, menu.*) pass through unchanged ─────────

The current `snaptap.co.za.conf` has a conflict between Nginx's static file serving and Nuxt's profile serving:

location / {

This means Nginx is trying to serve static profiles directly **before** they get to the Nuxt app. But the problem is:

2. Even if index.html exists, the CSS/JS assets are being looked for in the wrong place
3. The Nuxt middleware (`profiles.ts`) is designed to handle this, but Nginx intercepts first

### 2. Missing snaptap.me Configuration

The migration plan calls for a separate `/etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me` but it's not in the nginx folder being deployed.

### 3. Asset Path Issues

When profiles are served statically, relative asset paths (like `./css/style.css` or `./js/script.js`) need to resolve correctly. The Nginx config doesn't properly handle the static asset files within the profile directories.

### 4. Infinite Load on snaptap.me

Since there's no `snaptap.me` Nginx config, requests to snaptap.me might be:
- Falling through to a default server
- Getting caught in a redirect loop
- Not being proxied to the Nuxt app at all


---

The core issue is that Nginx's static file serving is conflicting with Nuxt's middleware, the `snaptap.me` domain lacks its own configuration entirely, and asset paths aren't resolving properly when profiles are served as static files. I should either let Nuxt handle all profile serving through its middleware or ensure Nginx properly proxies requests to the application instead of trying to serve files directly.

# SnapTap Profile Serving Troubleshooting Plan

## Current Issues Reported
1. `snaptap.co.za/checkeredblock` - can't access
2. `snaptap.co.za/sixdoor` - doesn't render correctly
3. `snaptap.co.za/siyanda` - doesn't render correctly
4. `snaptap.me/checkeredblock` - infinite load cycle

---

## Root Cause Analysis

### Issue 1: Conflicting Static File Handling (Nginx vs Nuxt)

The current `snaptap.co.za.conf` has a **conflict**:

```nginx
location / {
    root /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/;
    try_files /$uri/index.html /$uri.html @api_slug_fallback;
}
```

**Problem**: Nginx intercepts profile requests and tries to serve static files, but:
- Falls back to ASP.NET API (`@api_slug_fallback` → port 5000) instead of Nuxt
- Doesn't properly serve CSS/JS/images within profile directories
- The Nuxt `profiles.ts` middleware never gets the request

**Expected behavior from migration plan**: All traffic should go to Nuxt on port 3001, which then uses `profiles.ts` to serve static profiles from disk.

### Issue 2: Missing snaptap.me Nginx Configuration

The deployed `nginx/snaptap.co.za.conf` only covers `snaptap.co.za` subdomains. **There is no `snaptap.me.conf`** in the nginx deployment folder.

This explains the infinite load cycle on `snaptap.me/checkeredblock` - requests are either:
- Hitting a default Nginx server block
- Getting caught in a redirect loop
- Not being proxied anywhere

### Issue 3: Asset Path Resolution

Profile directories exist on VPS:
```
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/
  ├── ecwid/
  ├── images/
  ├── styles/
  └── mfb/
```

But the current Nginx config doesn't properly serve these nested assets. When `index.html` references `./styles/main.css`, the request for `/checkeredblock/styles/main.css` isn't being handled correctly.

---

## Recommended Fix

### Fix 1: Update snaptap.co.za.conf - Remove Static Serving, Proxy Everything to Nuxt

Replace the conflicting `location /` block with full Nuxt proxying:

```nginx
# Replace the current static file handling:
location / {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3001;
    proxy_http_version 1.1;
    proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
    proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
    proxy_set_header Host $host;
    proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
}

# Remove @api_slug_fallback - Nuxt handles unknown slugs
```

This lets the Nuxt `profiles.ts` middleware handle static profile serving with proper asset paths.

### Fix 2: Create and Deploy snaptap.me.conf

Create `/etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me`:

```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me;
    return 301 https://$host$request_uri;
}

server {
    listen 443 ssl;
    server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me;

    ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/privkey.pem;
    include /etc/letsencrypt/options-ssl-nginx.conf;
    ssl_dhparam /etc/letsencrypt/ssl-dhparams.pem;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3001;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
    }
}
```

### Fix 3: Verify Profile Structure

Each profile directory needs an `index.html` file:
```
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/index.html  ← Required
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/sixdoor/index.html         ← Required
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/siyanda/index.html         ← Required
```

---

## Diagnostic Commands to Run on VPS

```bash
# 1. Check if snaptap-frontend is running on port 3001
pm2 status
curl -I http://127.0.0.1:3001/

# 2. Check if profiles have index.html
ls -la /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/
ls -la /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/sixdoor/
ls -la /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/siyanda/

# 3. Check Nginx configs currently enabled
ls -la /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
cat /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap

# 4. Check if snaptap.me cert exists
ls -la /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/

# 5. Test Nginx config
sudo nginx -t

# 6. Check Nginx error logs
sudo tail -50 /var/nginx/error.log
sudo tail -50 /var/log/nginx/error.log

# 7. Check PM2 logs for errors
pm2 logs snaptap-frontend --lines 50
```

---

## VPS Access Options

Yes, you can grant me access to investigate directly. There are two options:

### Option A: SSH MCP Server (Recommended)
Install an SSH MCP server that gives me terminal access:
```bash
# On your machine, add to Claude Code MCP config:
# Uses https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-quickstarts/tree/main/mcp-server-ssh
```

### Option B: Share Terminal Output
Run the diagnostic commands above and paste the output here.

### Option C: Remote Desktop/Screen Share
If using VS Code Remote SSH, I can help via that session.

---

## Files to Update

| File | Status | Action |
|------|--------|--------|
| `nginx/snaptap.co.za.conf` | Needs update | Remove static serving, proxy all to Nuxt |
| `nginx/snaptap.me.conf` | Missing | Create new file |
| Profile directories | Check required | Verify `index.html` exists in each |

---

## Verification After Fixes

```bash
# Test profile serving
curl -sI https://snaptap.co.za/checkeredblock | head -5
curl -sI https://snaptap.co.za/sixdoor | head -5
curl -sI https://snaptap.me/checkeredblock | head -5

# Test asset loading
curl -sI https://snaptap.co.za/checkeredblock/styles/main.css | head -5
```

# SnapTap Profile Serving Troubleshooting Plan

## Current Issues Reported
1. `snaptap.co.za/checkeredblock` - can't access
2. `snaptap.co.za/sixdoor` - doesn't render correctly
3. `snaptap.co.za/siyanda` - doesn't render correctly
4. `snaptap.me/checkeredblock` - infinite load cycle

---

## Root Cause Analysis

### Issue 1: Conflicting Static File Handling (Nginx vs Nuxt)

The current `snaptap.co.za.conf` has a **conflict**:

```nginx
location / {
    root /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/;
    try_files /$uri/index.html /$uri.html @api_slug_fallback;
}
```

**Problem**: Nginx intercepts profile requests and tries to serve static files, but:
- Falls back to ASP.NET API (`@api_slug_fallback` → port 5000) instead of Nuxt
- Doesn't properly serve CSS/JS/images within profile directories
- The Nuxt `profiles.ts` middleware never gets the request

**Expected behavior from migration plan**: All traffic should go to Nuxt on port 3001, which then uses `profiles.ts` to serve static profiles from disk.

### Issue 2: Missing snaptap.me Nginx Configuration

The deployed `nginx/snaptap.co.za.conf` only covers `snaptap.co.za` subdomains. **There is no `snaptap.me.conf`** in the nginx deployment folder.

This explains the infinite load cycle on `snaptap.me/checkeredblock` - requests are either:
- Hitting a default Nginx server block
- Getting caught in a redirect loop
- Not being proxied anywhere

### Issue 3: Asset Path Resolution

Profile directories exist on VPS:
```
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/
  ├── ecwid/
  ├── images/
  ├── styles/
  └── mfb/
```

But the current Nginx config doesn't properly serve these nested assets. When `index.html` references `./styles/main.css`, the request for `/checkeredblock/styles/main.css` isn't being handled correctly.

---

## Recommended Fix

### Fix 1: Update snaptap.co.za.conf - Remove Static Serving, Proxy Everything to Nuxt

Replace the conflicting `location /` block with full Nuxt proxying:

```nginx
# Replace the current static file handling:
location / {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3001;
    proxy_http_version 1.1;
    proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
    proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
    proxy_set_header Host $host;
    proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
}

# Remove @api_slug_fallback - Nuxt handles unknown slugs
```

This lets the Nuxt `profiles.ts` middleware handle static profile serving with proper asset paths.

### Fix 2: Create and Deploy snaptap.me.conf

Create `/etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me`:

```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me;
    return 301 https://$host$request_uri;
}

server {
    listen 443 ssl;
    server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me;

    ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/privkey.pem;
    include /etc/letsencrypt/options-ssl-nginx.conf;
    ssl_dhparam /etc/letsencrypt/ssl-dhparams.pem;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3001;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
    }
}
```

### Fix 3: Verify Profile Structure

Each profile directory needs an `index.html` file:
```
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/index.html  ← Required
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/sixdoor/index.html         ← Required
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/siyanda/index.html         ← Required
```

---

## Diagnostic Commands to Run on VPS

```bash
# 1. Check if snaptap-frontend is running on port 3001
pm2 status
curl -I http://127.0.0.1:3001/

# 2. Check if profiles have index.html
ls -la /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/
ls -la /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/sixdoor/
ls -la /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/siyanda/

# 3. Check Nginx configs currently enabled
ls -la /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
cat /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap

# 4. Check if snaptap.me cert exists
ls -la /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/

# 5. Test Nginx config
sudo nginx -t

# 6. Check Nginx error logs
sudo tail -50 /var/nginx/error.log
sudo tail -50 /var/log/nginx/error.log

# 7. Check PM2 logs for errors
pm2 logs snaptap-frontend --lines 50
```

---

## VPS Access Options

Yes, you can grant me access to investigate directly. There are two options:

### Option A: SSH MCP Server (Recommended)
Install an SSH MCP server that gives me terminal access:
```bash
# On your machine, add to Claude Code MCP config:
# Uses https://github.com/anthropics/anthropic-quickstarts/tree/main/mcp-server-ssh
```

### Option B: Share Terminal Output
Run the diagnostic commands above and paste the output here.

### Option C: Remote Desktop/Screen Share
If using VS Code Remote SSH, I can help via that session.

---

## Files to Update

| File | Status | Action |
|------|--------|--------|
| `nginx/snaptap.co.za.conf` | Needs update | Remove static serving, proxy all to Nuxt |
| `nginx/snaptap.me.conf` | Missing | Create new file |
| Profile directories | Check required | Verify `index.html` exists in each |

---

## Verification After Fixes

```bash
# Test profile serving
curl -sI https://snaptap.co.za/checkeredblock | head -5
curl -sI https://snaptap.co.za/sixdoor | head -5
curl -sI https://snaptap.me/checkeredblock | head -5

# Test asset loading
curl -sI https://snaptap.co.za/checkeredblock/styles/main.css | head -5
```

# SnapTap Profile Serving Troubleshooting Plan

**Created**: 2026-03-03
**Reference**: Use across devices for debugging over coming days

## Current Issues Reported
1. `snaptap.co.za/checkeredblock` - can't access
2. `snaptap.co.za/sixdoor` - doesn't render correctly
3. `snaptap.co.za/siyanda` - doesn't render correctly
4. `snaptap.me/checkeredblock` - infinite load cycle

## Confirmed Facts
- ✅ Profile directories have `index.html` files
- ✅ SSL certificate for `snaptap.me` is issued
- ✅ Nuxt app running on port 3001 (`snaptap-frontend`)
- ❌ `snaptap.me` nginx config is **missing**
- ❌ `snaptap.co.za.conf` routing is **incorrect** (falls back to API instead of Nuxt)

---

## Root Cause Analysis

### Issue 1: Conflicting Static File Handling (Nginx vs Nuxt)

The current `snaptap.co.za.conf` has a **conflict**:

```nginx
location / {
    root /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/;
    try_files /$uri/index.html /$uri.html @api_slug_fallback;
}
```

**Problem**: Nginx intercepts profile requests and tries to serve static files, but:
- Falls back to ASP.NET API (`@api_slug_fallback` → port 5000) instead of Nuxt
- Doesn't properly serve CSS/JS/images within profile directories
- The Nuxt `profiles.ts` middleware never gets the request

**Expected behavior from migration plan**: All traffic should go to Nuxt on port 3001, which then uses `profiles.ts` to serve static profiles from disk.

### Issue 2: Missing snaptap.me Nginx Configuration

The deployed `nginx/snaptap.co.za.conf` only covers `snaptap.co.za` subdomains. **There is no `snaptap.me.conf`** in the nginx deployment folder.

This explains the infinite load cycle on `snaptap.me/checkeredblock` - requests are either:
- Hitting a default Nginx server block
- Getting caught in a redirect loop
- Not being proxied anywhere

### Issue 3: Asset Path Resolution

Profile directories exist on VPS:
```
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/
  ├── ecwid/
  ├── images/
  ├── styles/
  └── mfb/
```

But the current Nginx config doesn't properly serve these nested assets. When `index.html` references `./styles/main.css`, the request for `/checkeredblock/styles/main.css` isn't being handled correctly.

---

## Recommended Fix

### Fix 1: Update snaptap.co.za.conf - Remove Static Serving, Proxy Everything to Nuxt

Replace the conflicting `location /` block with full Nuxt proxying:

```nginx
# Replace the current static file handling:
location / {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3001;
    proxy_http_version 1.1;
    proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
    proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
    proxy_set_header Host $host;
    proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
}

# Remove @api_slug_fallback - Nuxt handles unknown slugs
```

This lets the Nuxt `profiles.ts` middleware handle static profile serving with proper asset paths.

### Fix 2: Create and Deploy snaptap.me.conf

Create `/etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me`:

```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me;
    return 301 https://$host$request_uri;
}

server {
    listen 443 ssl;
    server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me;

    ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/privkey.pem;
    include /etc/letsencrypt/options-ssl-nginx.conf;
    ssl_dhparam /etc/letsencrypt/ssl-dhparams.pem;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3001;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
    }
}
```

### Fix 3: Verify Profile Structure

Each profile directory needs an `index.html` file:
```
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/index.html  ← Required
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/sixdoor/index.html         ← Required
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/siyanda/index.html         ← Required
```

---

## Diagnostic Commands to Run on VPS

```bash
# 1. Check if snaptap-frontend is running on port 3001
pm2 status
curl -I http://127.0.0.1:3001/

# 2. Check if profiles have index.html
ls -la /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/
ls -la /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/sixdoor/
ls -la /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/siyanda/

# 3. Check Nginx configs currently enabled
ls -la /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
cat /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap

# 4. Check if snaptap.me cert exists
ls -la /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/

# 5. Test Nginx config
sudo nginx -t

# 6. Check Nginx error logs
sudo tail -50 /var/nginx/error.log
sudo tail -50 /var/log/nginx/error.log

# 7. Check PM2 logs for errors
pm2 logs snaptap-frontend --lines 50
```

---

## SSH MCP Server Setup (For Direct VPS Access)

To give me SSH access to investigate directly, set up an SSH MCP server:

### Step 1: Install the SSH MCP Server

```bash
# Option A: Using npx (easiest)
npx @anthropic-ai/mcp-server-ssh

# Option B: Install globally
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/mcp-server-ssh
```

Add to your Claude Code MCP config (`.claude/mcp.json` or via settings):

# MCP SSH Agent Installation & Configuration Guide

## Installation Methods

### Desktop Extension (Recommended)
Download the latest `.dxt` file from [GitHub releases](https://github.com/aiondadotcom/mcp-ssh/releases) and double-click to install in Claude Desktop.

### Via npx
```bash
npx @aiondadotcom/mcp-ssh
```

### Global Installation
```bash
npm install -g @aiondadotcom/mcp-ssh
```

### Local Development
```bash
git clone https://github.com/aiondadotcom/mcp-ssh.git
cd mcp-ssh
npm install
npm start
```

## Claude Desktop Configuration

**macOS location:** `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`

**Windows location:** `%APPDATA%/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "mcp-ssh": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@aiondadotcom/mcp-ssh"]
    }
  }
}
```

## Environment Variables

- `MCP_SILENT=true` — Disables debug output (automatically set when running as MCP server)
- `MCP_SILENT=false` — Enables debug mode for detailed operation logs

## Requirements

- Node.js 18 or higher
- SSH client installed (`ssh` and `scp` commands available)
- SSH configuration files at `~/.ssh/config` and `~/.ssh/known_hosts`

## Key Features

The server provides these MCP tools:

1. **listKnownHosts()** — Lists SSH hosts from config and known_hosts
2. **runRemoteCommand(hostAlias, command)** — Executes remote commands via SSH
3. **getHostInfo(hostAlias)** — Returns host configuration details
4. **checkConnectivity(hostAlias)** — Tests SSH connectivity
5. **uploadFile(hostAlias, localPath, remotePath)** — Uploads files via SCP
6. **downloadFile(hostAlias, remotePath, localPath)** — Downloads files via SCP
7. **runCommandBatch(hostAlias, commands)** — Executes multiple commands sequentially

All operations use native SSH tools for maximum reliability and full authentication method support.

# SnapTap Profile Serving Troubleshooting Plan

**Created**: 2026-03-03
**Reference**: Use across devices for debugging over coming days
**Save Location**: Will be saved to repo as `SNAPTAP_TROUBLESHOOTING.md` alongside `SNAPTAP_MIGRATION_PLAN.md`

## Current Issues Reported
1. `snaptap.co.za/checkeredblock` - can't access
2. `snaptap.co.za/sixdoor` - doesn't render correctly
3. `snaptap.co.za/siyanda` - doesn't render correctly
4. `snaptap.me/checkeredblock` - infinite load cycle

## Confirmed Facts
- ✅ Profile directories have `index.html` files
- ✅ SSL certificate for `snaptap.me` is issued
- ✅ Nuxt app running on port 3001 (`snaptap-frontend`)
- ❌ `snaptap.me` nginx config is **missing**
- ❌ `snaptap.co.za.conf` routing is **incorrect** (falls back to API instead of Nuxt)

---

## Root Cause Analysis

### Issue 1: Conflicting Static File Handling (Nginx vs Nuxt)

The current `snaptap.co.za.conf` has a **conflict**:

```nginx
location / {
    root /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/;
    try_files /$uri/index.html /$uri.html @api_slug_fallback;
}
```

**Problem**: Nginx intercepts profile requests and tries to serve static files, but:
- Falls back to ASP.NET API (`@api_slug_fallback` → port 5000) instead of Nuxt
- Doesn't properly serve CSS/JS/images within profile directories
- The Nuxt `profiles.ts` middleware never gets the request

**Expected behavior from migration plan**: All traffic should go to Nuxt on port 3001, which then uses `profiles.ts` to serve static profiles from disk.

### Issue 2: Missing snaptap.me Nginx Configuration

The deployed `nginx/snaptap.co.za.conf` only covers `snaptap.co.za` subdomains. **There is no `snaptap.me.conf`** in the nginx deployment folder.

This explains the infinite load cycle on `snaptap.me/checkeredblock` - requests are either:
- Hitting a default Nginx server block
- Getting caught in a redirect loop
- Not being proxied anywhere

### Issue 3: Asset Path Resolution

Profile directories exist on VPS:
```
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/
  ├── ecwid/
  ├── images/
  ├── styles/
  └── mfb/
```

But the current Nginx config doesn't properly serve these nested assets. When `index.html` references `./styles/main.css`, the request for `/checkeredblock/styles/main.css` isn't being handled correctly.

---

## Recommended Fix

### Fix 1: Update snaptap.co.za.conf - Remove Static Serving, Proxy Everything to Nuxt

Replace the conflicting `location /` block with full Nuxt proxying:

```nginx
# Replace the current static file handling:
location / {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3001;
    proxy_http_version 1.1;
    proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
    proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
    proxy_set_header Host $host;
    proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
}

# Remove @api_slug_fallback - Nuxt handles unknown slugs
```

This lets the Nuxt `profiles.ts` middleware handle static profile serving with proper asset paths.

### Fix 2: Create and Deploy snaptap.me.conf

Create `/etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me`:

```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me;
    return 301 https://$host$request_uri;
}

server {
    listen 443 ssl;
    server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me;

    ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/privkey.pem;
    include /etc/letsencrypt/options-ssl-nginx.conf;
    ssl_dhparam /etc/letsencrypt/ssl-dhparams.pem;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3001;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
    }
}
```

### Fix 3: Verify Profile Structure

Each profile directory needs an `index.html` file:
```
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/index.html  ← Required
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/sixdoor/index.html         ← Required
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/siyanda/index.html         ← Required
```

---

## Diagnostic Commands to Run on VPS

```bash

pm2 status
curl -I http://127.0.0.1:3001/

# 2. Check if profiles have index.html

# 5. Test Nginx config
sudo nginx -t

# 6. Check Nginx error logs
sudo tail -50 /var/nginx/error.log
sudo tail -50 /var/log/nginx/error.log

# 7. Check PM2 logs for errors
pm2 logs snaptap-frontend --lines 50
```

---

# SnapTap Profile Serving Troubleshooting Plan

**Created**: 2026-03-03
**Reference**: Use across devices for debugging over coming days
**Save Location**: Will be saved to repo as `SNAPTAP_TROUBLESHOOTING.md` alongside `SNAPTAP_MIGRATION_PLAN.md`

## Current Issues Reported
1. `snaptap.co.za/checkeredblock` - can't access
2. `snaptap.co.za/sixdoor` - doesn't render correctly
3. `snaptap.co.za/siyanda` - doesn't render correctly
4. `snaptap.me/checkeredblock` - infinite load cycle

## Confirmed Facts
- ✅ Profile directories have `index.html` files
- ✅ SSL certificate for `snaptap.me` is issued
- ✅ Nuxt app running on port 3001 (`snaptap-frontend`)
- ❌ `snaptap.me` nginx config is **missing**
- ❌ `snaptap.co.za.conf` routing is **incorrect** (falls back to API instead of Nuxt)

---

## Root Cause Analysis

### Issue 1: Conflicting Static File Handling (Nginx vs Nuxt)

The current `snaptap.co.za.conf` has a **conflict**:

```nginx
location / {
    root /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/;
    try_files /$uri/index.html /$uri.html @api_slug_fallback;
}
```

**Problem**: Nginx intercepts profile requests and tries to serve static files, but:
- Falls back to ASP.NET API (`@api_slug_fallback` → port 5000) instead of Nuxt
- Doesn't properly serve CSS/JS/images within profile directories
- The Nuxt `profiles.ts` middleware never gets the request

**Expected behavior from migration plan**: All traffic should go to Nuxt on port 3001, which then uses `profiles.ts` to serve static profiles from disk.

### Issue 2: Missing snaptap.me Nginx Configuration

The deployed `nginx/snaptap.co.za.conf` only covers `snaptap.co.za` subdomains. **There is no `snaptap.me.conf`** in the nginx deployment folder.

This explains the infinite load cycle on `snaptap.me/checkeredblock` - requests are either:
- Hitting a default Nginx server block
- Getting caught in a redirect loop
- Not being proxied anywhere

### Issue 3: Asset Path Resolution

Profile directories exist on VPS:
```
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/
  ├── ecwid/
  ├── images/
  ├── styles/
  └── mfb/
```

But the current Nginx config doesn't properly serve these nested assets. When `index.html` references `./styles/main.css`, the request for `/checkeredblock/styles/main.css` isn't being handled correctly.

---

## Recommended Fix

### Fix 1: Update snaptap.co.za.conf - Remove Static Serving, Proxy Everything to Nuxt

Replace the conflicting `location /` block with full Nuxt proxying:

```nginx
# Replace the current static file handling:
location / {
    proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3001;
    proxy_http_version 1.1;
    proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
    proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
    proxy_set_header Host $host;
    proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
    proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
    proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
}

# Remove @api_slug_fallback - Nuxt handles unknown slugs
```

This lets the Nuxt `profiles.ts` middleware handle static profile serving with proper asset paths.

### Fix 2: Create and Deploy snaptap.me.conf

Create `/etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap-me`:

```nginx
server {
    listen 80;
    server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me;
    return 301 https://$host$request_uri;
}

server {
    listen 443 ssl;
    server_name snaptap.me www.snaptap.me cards.snaptap.me menu.snaptap.me;

    ssl_certificate /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/fullchain.pem;
    ssl_certificate_key /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/privkey.pem;
    include /etc/letsencrypt/options-ssl-nginx.conf;
    ssl_dhparam /etc/letsencrypt/ssl-dhparams.pem;

    location / {
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:3001;
        proxy_http_version 1.1;
        proxy_set_header Upgrade $http_upgrade;
        proxy_set_header Connection 'upgrade';
        proxy_set_header Host $host;
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;
        proxy_cache_bypass $http_upgrade;
    }
}
```

### Fix 3: Verify Profile Structure

Each profile directory needs an `index.html` file:
```
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/index.html  ← Required
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/sixdoor/index.html         ← Required
/var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/siyanda/index.html         ← Required
```

---

## Diagnostic Commands to Run on VPS

```bash
# 1. Check if snaptap-frontend is running on port 3001
pm2 status
curl -I http://127.0.0.1:3001/

# 2. Check if profiles have index.html
ls -la /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/checkeredblock/
ls -la /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/sixdoor/
ls -la /var/www/snaptap/ui/profiles/siyanda/

# 3. Check Nginx configs currently enabled
ls -la /etc/nginx/sites-enabled/
cat /etc/nginx/sites-available/snaptap

# 4. Check if snaptap.me cert exists
ls -la /etc/letsencrypt/live/snaptap.me/

# 5. Test Nginx config
sudo nginx -t

# 6. Check Nginx error logs
sudo tail -50 /var/nginx/error.log
sudo tail -50 /var/log/nginx/error.log

# 7. Check PM2 logs for errors
pm2 logs snaptap-frontend --lines 50
```

## SSH MCP Server Setup (For Direct VPS Access)

To give me SSH access to investigate directly, set up an SSH MCP server.

**Package**: `@aiondadotcom/mcp-ssh` ([GitHub](https://github.com/AiondaDotCom/mcp-ssh))

### Step 1: Set Up Your SSH Config

First, add your VPS to `~/.ssh/config`:

```
Host snaptap-vps
    HostName 154.66.198.20
    User your-username
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/your-private-key
```

Test the connection:
```bash
ssh snaptap-vps "echo 'Connection successful'"
```

### Step 2: Configure Claude Code MCP

**Windows**: Edit `%APPDATA%\Claude\claude_desktop_config.json`
**macOS**: Edit `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json`
**Claude Code**: Edit `.claude/mcp.json` in your project or user config

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "mcp-ssh": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@aiondadotcom/mcp-ssh"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Step 3: Restart Claude Code

Restart Claude Code after adding the config. The MCP server reads your `~/.ssh/config` and `~/.ssh/known_hosts` automatically.

I'll be able to use:
